user_query:
I’m trying to cut back on fast-fashion and want a small, script-driven plan I can run locally. Please do the following:

1) Use a search engine to identify and record official, organization-backed information relevant to more sustainable apparel choices:
   - Compile at least 5 apparel-related sustainability certifications from their official organizations (e.g., standard owners or accreditation bodies). For each, record: name, owning organization, the organization’s canonical domain (no deep links), scope keywords (e.g., material/process/social/environmental), and a short one-sentence note.
   - Compile at least 3 official care/repair/resale guidance sources from recognized organizations (e.g., standards bodies, public agencies, or non-profit initiatives). For each, record: title, organization, the organization’s canonical domain, topic (care|repair|resale|rental), and a short one-sentence note.
   - Save the exact search queries you used (one per line) and the chosen result domains to: data/references/queries.log (plain text).
   - Save the certifications to: data/references/certifications.json (array of objects with fields: name, organization, domain, scope, note).
   - Save the care/repair guidance to: data/references/care_guides.json (array of objects with fields: title, organization, domain, topic, note).
   Do not include direct URLs in these files, only the canonical domains (e.g., organization.org).

2) Build a command-line script that ingests my purchase history and the above references, then writes structured outputs under output/. The script must be runnable from the shell and accept these arguments:
   - --purchases input/purchases.csv
   - --certs data/references/certifications.json
   - --guides data/references/care_guides.json
   - --out-dir output/

   The script should:
   - Interpret total_items as the sum of the qty column.
   - Compute baseline metrics and write two CSVs:
     a) output/baseline_brands.csv with columns: brand,total_items,total_spend,likely_fast_fashion_items
     b) output/baseline_materials.csv with columns: material,total_items
   - For the purpose of this exercise, define likely_fast_fashion_items as items where price_usd <= 25 AND material is one of ["polyester","acrylic","nylon","polyamide"]. Count items by qty.
   - Produce a structured decision architecture at output/targets_and_rules.json with:
     {
       "baseline_summary": {"total_items": number, "total_spend": number, "likely_fast_fashion_items": number},
       "reduction_targets": {"items_per_month_max": number, "polyester_share_max": number (0-1), "annual_spend_cap": number},
       "purchase_rules": [
         {"id": string, "category": "materials"|"certifications"|"care"|"resale"|"budget", "description": string, "references": [strings referencing names from certifications.json or organizations from care_guides.json], "expected_impact": ["lower_emissions"|"extend_lifespan"|"reduce_spend"]}
       ]
     }
     Include at least 5 purchase_rules total, with at least: one materials-related rule, one certification-related rule, and one care/repair/resale-related rule. References must match entries you saved in certifications.json or care_guides.json by name or organization.
   - Generate a readable summary report at output/report.md with clearly labeled sections: Baseline, Targets, Rules, Checklist, References. In Baseline, report total_items, total_spend, and likely_fast_fashion_items. In References, state how many certifications and care guides were loaded and list their organizations.

3) Run the script from the command line using the provided input file paths and write all outputs to the specified locations. Ensure that data/references/*.json are actually read by the script (not hardcoded). All outputs must be reproducible by re-running the same command.

Notes and constraints:
- Do not include or hardcode any direct URLs. Only record canonical domains and organization names for web-sourced references.
- Keep assumptions explicit in the report (e.g., the likely_fast_fashion heuristic above).
- Do not require any credentials or paid services.

Input path: input/purchases.csv
Required output paths: data/references/queries.log, data/references/certifications.json, data/references/care_guides.json, scripts/wardrobe_plan.py (or equivalent executable script), output/baseline_brands.csv, output/baseline_materials.csv, output/targets_and_rules.json, output/report.md All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
